title: rubus granulatus subsp. misniensis
intro: Rubus granulatus subsp. misniensis, also known as the Kashmir raspberry or Himalayan raspberry, is a subspecies of the Rubus granulatus plant, a member of the rose family that is native to the Himalayan region. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and is traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Rubus granulatus subsp. misniensis include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive problems, and support the immune system. The plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and can also be used to make jams and other herbal preparations.
